ddls:
CREATE TABLE "attack_methods" (
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attack method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the attack method */,
  "denial_of_service" INTEGER /* Indicates if it is a Denial of Service attack (Y/N) */,
  "replay_attack" INTEGER /* Indicates if it is a Replay attack (Y/N) */,
  "sniffing" INTEGER /* Indicates if it involves Sniffing (Y/N) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attack method */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the attack (e.g., Network, Application, Social Engineering) */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the attack (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of last update to the attack method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prevention_techniques" (
  "technique_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prevention technique */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "technique_name" TEXT /* Name of the prevention technique */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the technique */,
  "implementation_cost" TEXT /* Estimated implementation cost of the technique */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technique_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prevention_techniques_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "node_replication" (
  "replication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each replication event */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node involved in replication */,
  "replication_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if replication is active during the attack */,
  "replication_date" TEXT /* Date of replication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("replication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_node_replication_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attack_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the attack occurrence */,
  "result" TEXT /* Outcome of the attack (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the attack */,
  "affected_systems" TEXT /* List of affected systems */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attack_logs_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., Security, Research) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to users */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view attack method, download report) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed by the user (e.g., attack method, report) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerabilities" (
  "vulnerability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vulnerability */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "vulnerability_name" TEXT /* Name of the known vulnerability */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the vulnerability */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the vulnerability (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "exploitability" TEXT /* Exploitability of the vulnerability (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vulnerability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerabilities_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the attachment file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the attachment file */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the attachment was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the attachment file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attachments_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countermeasures" (
  "countermeasure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each countermeasure */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the countermeasure */,
  "effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of the countermeasure (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "implementation_complexity" TEXT /* Implementation complexity of the countermeasure (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("countermeasure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countermeasures_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "research_papers" (
  "paper_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each research paper */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "paper_title" TEXT /* Title of the research paper */,
  "paper_author" TEXT /* Author of the research paper */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date of publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("paper_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_research_papers_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attack_simulations" (
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each simulation */,
  "attack_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to attack methods */,
  "simulation_date" TEXT /* Date of simulation */,
  "simulation_result" TEXT /* Result of simulation (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "simulation_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of simulation results */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attack_simulations_attack_id FOREIGN KEY ("attack_id") REFERENCES attack_methods ("attack_id")
)
